content:
Post 1
ASX 200 closed at 8,653.30, up 49.10 points or 0.57%, crossing back above its 20-day moving average. The session was driven by a rotation into consumer names, with defensives and rate-sensitive sectors leading. The index remains virtually unchanged year to date despite a 1.51% five-day loss.

Post 2
Consumer Staples led all sectors at +3.87%, while Information Technology was the clear laggard at -2.34%. SDF surged 36.20% to AUD 5.38 after Steadfast Group drew strong buying interest, with AUB Group adding 9.84% to AUD 28.70 in a broad re-rating of the insurance broker space.

Post 3
Gold at AUD 5,971.18 per oz is the key level to watch. With Materials down 1.14% and the AUD sitting at 0.7014, any further USD strength that pushes gold below AUD 5,900 per oz would signal renewed pressure on the resources sector and likely weigh on the index early next week.

Post 1
ASX 200 closed at 8,653.30, up 49.10 points or 0.57%, reclaiming its 20-day moving average. The session was driven by a sharp rotation into consumer names, with defensives and rate-sensitive sectors leading while tech and materials sold off hard.

Post 2
Consumer Staples led all sectors at +3.87%, with Consumer Discretionary close behind at +3.58%. Information Technology was the clear laggard, falling 2.34%. SDF surged 36.20% to AUD 5.38 on a takeover-driven catalyst, while Liontown shed 7.97% as lithium sentiment continued to weigh.

Post 3
Materials fell 1.14% and Energy dropped 0.87% Wednesday. Gold at AUD 5,954.28 per oz is the commodity level to watch — any pullback here will pressure the materials complex further. With the ASX down 1.51% over five days, the index needs to hold above 8,600 to confirm this bounce has legs.
